actions.c(3) Library Functions Manual actions.c(3)

NAME

actions.c - Action option callbacks for ascii-chat.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void actions_defer (deferred_action_t action, const action_args_t *args)
Set which action to defer until options are fully initialized. deferred_action_t actions_get_deferred (void)
Get the deferred action (if any) that was set. const action_args_t * actions_get_args (void)
Get the arguments for the deferred action. void action_list_webcams (void)
List available webcam devices and exit. void action_list_microphones (void)
List available microphone devices and exit. void action_list_speakers (void)
List available speaker devices and exit. void action_show_capabilities_immediate (void)
Execute show capabilities immediately (for early binary-level execution). void action_show_capabilities (void)
Show terminal capabilities and exit. void action_check_update_immediate (void)
Execute update check immediately (for early binary-level execution). void action_check_update (void)
Check for updates and display results. void action_show_version (void)
Show version information and exit. void action_help_server (void)
Show server mode help and exit. void action_help_client (void)
Show client mode help and exit. void action_help_mirror (void)
Show mirror mode help and exit. void action_help_acds (void)
Show discovery-service mode help and exit. void action_help_discovery (void)
Show discovery mode help and exit. void action_create_manpage (const char *output_path)
Generate man page template from options builder. void action_create_config (const char *output_path)
Create default configuration file and exit. void action_completions (const char *shell_name, const char *output_path)
Generate shell completions and output to stdout or file. void actions_execute_deferred (void)
Execute the deferred action (if any).

Detailed Description

Action option callbacks for ascii-chat.

Action options are deferred until after all options are fully parsed and initialized. This ensures that options like --width and --height are properly reflected in action output (e.g., --show-capabilities displays the final terminal dimensions).

Examples: --list-webcams, --list-microphones, --list-speakers, --show-capabilities

Definition in file options/parsing/actions.c.

Function Documentation

void action_check_update (void )

Check for updates and display results. Performs update check against GitHub releases API and displays results. Exits with code 0 on success, 1 on error.

Definition at line 329 of file options/parsing/actions.c.

329                                {
330   // Defer execution until after options are fully parsed
331   actions_defer(ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE, NULL);
332 }




References ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE, and
  actions_defer().


Referenced by options_registry_add_all_to_builder().






void
  action_check_update_immediate (void )


Execute update check immediately (for early binary-level
    execution). Execute update check immediately (for binary-level
  execution).


Definition at line 311 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


311                                          {
312   printf("Checking for updates...\n");
313   update_check_result_t result;
314   asciichat_error_t err = update_check_perform(&result);
315   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
316     printf("\nFailed to check for updates.\n\n");
317     action_exit(1);
318   }
319   if (result.update_available) {
320     char notification[1024];
321     update_check_format_notification(&result, notification, sizeof(notification));
322     printf("\n%s\n\n", notification);
323   } else {
324     printf("\nYou are already on the latest version: %s (%.8s)\n\n", result.current_version, result.current_sha);
325   }
326   action_exit(0);
327 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    update_check_result_t::current_sha,
    update_check_result_t::current_version,
    update_check_result_t::update_available,
    update_check_format_notification(), and
    update_check_perform().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_completions (const char * shell_name, const char *
  output_path)


Generate shell completions and output to stdout or file. Generates
    shell completion script for the specified shell (bash, fish, zsh,
    powershell). Output is dynamically generated from the options registry,
    ensuring completions are always in sync with current options. Outputs to
    stdout by default, or to specified file path if provided. Prompts for
    overwrite confirmation if file already exists. Exits with code 0 on success,
    1 on error.




Parameters


shell_name Shell name: "bash",
  "fish", "zsh", or "powershell"


output_path Optional file path to write completions to. NULL or empty
  string = stdout.


Definition at line 571 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


571                                                                          {
572   if (!shell_name || strlen(shell_name) == 0) {
573     log_error("--completions requires shell name (bash, fish, zsh, powershell)");
574     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
575   }
576 
577   completion_format_t format = completions_parse_shell_name(shell_name);
578   if (format == COMPLETION_FORMAT_UNKNOWN) {
579     log_error("Unknown shell '%s' (supported: bash, fish, zsh, powershell)", shell_name);
580     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
581   }
582 
583   FILE *output = stdout;
584   bool should_close = false;
585 
586   // Determine output: use provided path if given and not "-", otherwise stdout
587   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
588     // Check if file already exists and prompt for confirmation
589     struct stat st;
590     if (stat(output_path, &st) == 0) {
591       // File exists - ask user if they want to overwrite
592       log_plain("Completions file already exists: %s", output_path);
593 
594       bool overwrite = platform_prompt_yes_no("Overwrite", false); // Default to No
595       if (!overwrite) {
596         log_plain("Completions generation cancelled.");
597         action_exit(0);
598       }
599 
600       log_plain("Overwriting existing completions file...");
601     }
602 
603     output = platform_fopen("file_stream", output_path, "w");
604     if (!output) {
605       log_error("Failed to open %s for writing", output_path);
606       action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
607     }
608     should_close = true;
609   }
610 
611   asciichat_error_t result = completions_generate_for_shell(format, output);
612 
613   if (should_close) {
614     fclose(output);
615   }
616 
617   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
618     log_error("Failed to generate %s completions", completions_get_shell_name(format));
619     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
620   }
621 
622   // Silently exit - completions are written (either to file or stdout)
623   action_exit(0);
624 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, COMPLETION_FORMAT_UNKNOWN,
    completions_generate_for_shell(),
    completions_get_shell_name(), completions_parse_shell_name(),
    ERROR_FILE_OPERATION, ERROR_USAGE, log_error,
    log_plain, platform_fopen(), and
    platform_prompt_yes_no().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_create_config (const char * output_path)


Create default configuration file and exit. Creates a default
    configuration file. Outputs to stdout by default, or to specified file path
    if provided. Prompts for overwrite confirmation if file already exists.
    Exits with code 0 on success, 1 on error.




Parameters


output_path Optional file path to write config to.
  NULL or empty string = stdout.


Definition at line 524 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


524                                                    {
525   // Build the schema first so config_create_default can generate options from it
526   const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
527   if (unified_config) {
528     asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
529     if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
530       // Schema build failed, but continue anyway
531       (void)schema_build_result;
532     }
533     // options_config_destroy(unified_config);
534   }
535 
536   const char *config_path = NULL;
537 
538   // Determine output path: use provided path, or NULL for stdout
539   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
540     // User provided explicit path - use it as-is
541     config_path = output_path;
542   } else {
543     // No path or "-" means stdout
544     config_path = NULL;
545   }
546 
547   // Create config with default options
548   asciichat_error_t result = config_create_default(config_path);
549   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
550     asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
551     if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
552       log_error("Error creating config: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
553     } else {
554       log_error("Failed to create config file");
555     }
556     action_exit(ERROR_CONFIG);
557   }
558 
559   if (config_path) {
560     log_info("Created default config file at: %s", config_path);
561   } else {
562     log_info("Config written to stdout");
563   }
564   action_exit(0);
565 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, config_create_default(),
    config_schema_build_from_configs(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message, ERROR_CONFIG,
    HAS_ERRNO, log_error, log_info, and
    options_preset_unified().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_create_manpage (const char * output_path)


Generate man page template from options builder. Generates a
    merged man page template preserving manual content. Outputs to stdout by
    default, or to specified file path if provided. Prompts for overwrite
    confirmation if file already exists. Exits with code 0 on success, 1 on
    error.




Parameters


output_path Optional file path to write man page
  to. NULL or empty string = stdout.


Definition at line 475 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


475                                                     {
476   // Get binary-level config
477   const options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
478 
479   if (!config) {
480     log_error("Failed to get binary options config");
481     action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
482   }
483 
484   // Determine output path: use provided path, or NULL for stdout
485   const char *path_to_use = NULL;
486   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
487     // User provided explicit path - use it
488     path_to_use = output_path;
489   } else {
490     // No path or "-" means stdout
491     path_to_use = NULL;
492   }
493 
494   // Generate merged man page from embedded or filesystem resources
495   // Resources are loaded automatically based on build type:
496   // - Production (Release): From embedded binary data
497   // - Development (Debug): From filesystem files
498   asciichat_error_t err =
499       options_config_generate_manpage_merged(config, "ascii-chat", NULL, path_to_use, "Video chat in your terminal");
500 
501   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
502     asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
503     if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
504       log_error("%s", err_ctx.context_message);
505     } else {
506       log_error("Failed to generate man page");
507     }
508     action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
509   }
510 
511   if (path_to_use) {
512     log_info("Man page written to: %s", path_to_use);
513   } else {
514     log_info("Man page written to stdout");
515   }
516 
517   action_exit(0);
518 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    ERROR_FILE_OPERATION, HAS_ERRNO, log_error,
    log_info, options_config_generate_manpage_merged(), and
    options_preset_unified().

void
  action_list_microphones (void )

    input devices and prints them to stdout. Exits with code 0 on success, 1 on
    error.

void
  action_show_capabilities (void )


Show terminal capabilities and exit. Detects and displays terminal
    color support, UTF-8 support, dimensions, and terminal program name. Exits
    with code 0.




Note: This action is deferred until after all options are parsed
    and dimensions are calculated, so that --width and --height flags are
    properly reflected in the output.


Definition at line 298 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


298                                     {
299   // Defer execution until after options are fully parsed and dimensions updated
300   // This ensures --width and --height flags are properly reflected in the output
301   actions_defer(ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES, NULL);
302 }






References ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES, and
    actions_defer().


Referenced by options_registry_add_all_to_builder().






void
  action_show_capabilities_immediate (void )


Execute show capabilities immediately (for early binary-level
    execution). Execute show capabilities immediately (for binary-level
    execution).


Definition at line 228 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


228                                               {
229   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
230 
231   // Determine if we should use colored output
232   bool use_colors = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
233 
234   // Override color level if colors should not be shown
235   if (!use_colors) {
236     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
237     caps.color_count = 0;
238     caps.capabilities &= ~((uint32_t)(TERM_CAP_COLOR_TRUE | TERM_CAP_COLOR_256 | TERM_CAP_COLOR_16));
239   }
240 
241   // Use detected terminal size (immediate execution doesn't have parsed options yet)
242   terminal_size_t size;
243   terminal_get_size(&size);
244   unsigned short width = size.cols;
245   unsigned short height = size.rows;
246 
247   log_color_t label_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_DEBUG : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
248   log_color_t string_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_DEBUG : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
249   log_color_t good_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_INFO : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
250   log_color_t bad_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_ERROR : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
251   log_color_t number_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_DEBUG : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
252 
253   printf("%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "Terminal Capabilities:"));
254 
255   char size_buf[64];
256   snprintf(size_buf, sizeof(size_buf), "%ux%u", width, height);
257   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Terminal Size"), colored_string(number_color, size_buf));
258 
259   const char *color_level_name = terminal_color_level_name(caps.color_level);
260   log_color_t color_level_color = (caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_NONE) ? bad_color : string_color;
261   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Color Level"),
262          colored_string(color_level_color, (char *)color_level_name));
263 
264   char colors_buf[64];
265   snprintf(colors_buf, sizeof(colors_buf), "%u", caps.color_count);
266   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Max Colors"), colored_string(number_color, colors_buf));
267 
268   log_color_t utf8_color = caps.utf8_support ? good_color : bad_color;
269   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "UTF-8 Support"),
270          colored_string(utf8_color, (char *)(caps.utf8_support ? "Yes" : "No")));
271 
272   const char *render_mode_str = "unknown";
273   if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND) {
274     render_mode_str = "foreground";
275   } else if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND) {
276     render_mode_str = "background";
277   } else if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) {
278     render_mode_str = "half-block";
279   }
280   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Render Mode"),
281          colored_string(string_color, (char *)render_mode_str));
282   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "TERM"), colored_string(string_color, (char *)caps.term_type));
283   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "COLORTERM"),
284          colored_string(string_color, (char *)(strlen(caps.colorterm) ? caps.colorterm : "(not set)")));
285 
286   log_color_t reliable_color = caps.detection_reliable ? good_color : bad_color;
287   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Detection Reliable"),
288          colored_string(reliable_color, (char *)(caps.detection_reliable ? "Yes" : "No")));
289 
290   char bitmask_buf[64];
291   snprintf(bitmask_buf, sizeof(bitmask_buf), "0x%08x", caps.capabilities);
292   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Capabilities Bitmask"), colored_string(number_color, bitmask_buf));
293 
294   fflush(stdout);
295   action_exit(0);
296 }






References terminal_capabilities_t::capabilities,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_count,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_level, colored_string(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::colorterm, terminal_size_t::cols,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::detection_reliable, LOG_COLOR_DEBUG,
    LOG_COLOR_ERROR, LOG_COLOR_GREY, LOG_COLOR_INFO,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, terminal_capabilities_t::render_mode,
    RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND, RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND,
    RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK, terminal_size_t::rows,
    TERM_CAP_COLOR_16, TERM_CAP_COLOR_256,
    TERM_CAP_COLOR_TRUE, TERM_COLOR_NONE,
    terminal_capabilities_t::term_type,
    terminal_color_level_name(), terminal_get_size(),
    terminal_should_color_output(), and
    terminal_capabilities_t::utf8_support.

void
  action_show_version (void )


Show version information and exit. Displays ascii-chat version,
    build type, build date, and compiler information. Exits with code 0.


Definition at line 428 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


428                                {
429   bool use_colors = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
430   if (use_colors) {
431     printf("%s %s (%s, %s)\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "ascii-chat"),
432            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL),
433            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE),
434            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE));
435   } else {
436     printf("ascii-chat %s (%s, %s)\n", ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL, ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE);
437   }
438 
439   action_exit(0);
440 }






References ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE,

    colored_string(), LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_GREY,
    LOG_COLOR_INFO, LOG_COLOR_WARN, and
    terminal_should_color_output().


Referenced by main().






void
  actions_defer (deferred_action_t action, const action_args_t *
  args)


Set which action to defer until options are fully initialized.
    Only the first action found is remembered. If multiple actions are
    specified, only the first one will be executed.




Parameters


action The action type to
  defer


args Optional action arguments (can be NULL)


Definition at line 75 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


75                                                                         {
76   // Only remember the first action found
77   if (g_deferred_action_state.has_action) {
78     return; // Action already deferred, ignore subsequent actions
79   }
80 
81   g_deferred_action_state.action = action;
82   g_deferred_action_state.has_action = true;
83 
84   // Copy arguments if provided
85   if (args) {
86     memcpy(&g_deferred_action_state.args, args, sizeof(action_args_t));
87   } else {
88     memset(&g_deferred_action_state.args, 0, sizeof(action_args_t));
89   }
90 }






References action, and args.


Referenced by action_check_update(),
    action_list_microphones(), action_list_speakers(),
    action_list_webcams(), and action_show_capabilities().






void
  actions_execute_deferred (void )


Execute the deferred action (if any). This function should be
    called during STAGE 8 of options_init() after all options are fully
    parsed and dimensions are calculated.


Definition at line 662 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


662                                     {
663   deferred_action_t action = actions_get_deferred();
664 
665   switch (action) {
666   case ACTION_NONE:
667     // No action to execute
668     break;
669 
670   case ACTION_LIST_WEBCAMS:
671     execute_list_webcams();
672     break;
673 
674   case ACTION_LIST_MICROPHONES:
675     execute_list_microphones();
676     break;
677 
678   case ACTION_LIST_SPEAKERS:
679     execute_list_speakers();
680     break;
681 
682   case ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES:
683     execute_show_capabilities();
684     break;
685 
686   case ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE:
687     execute_check_update();
688     break;
689 
690   default:
691     log_warn("Unknown deferred action: %d", action);
692     break;
693   }
694 }






References action, ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE,
    ACTION_LIST_MICROPHONES, ACTION_LIST_SPEAKERS,
    ACTION_LIST_WEBCAMS, ACTION_NONE,
    ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES, actions_get_deferred(), and
    log_warn.


Referenced by options_init().

    asciichat_fatal_with_context(),
    asciichat_set_errno_with_message(),
    asciichat_set_errno_with_system_error_and_message(),
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), format_message(),
    frame_buffer_printf(), log_file_msg(), log_labeled(),
    log_mmap_write(), log_msg(), log_net_message(),
    log_network_message(), log_plain_msg(),
    log_plain_stderr_msg(), log_plain_stderr_nonewline_msg(),
    log_plain_stdout_msg(), log_terminal_msg(), main(),
    named_register_fmt(), options_builder_add_example(),
    options_builder_add_example_utility(), platform_asprintf(),
    platform_open(), safe_fprintf(), and
  safe_snprintf().






bool has_action
